functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.check_event_attributes
documentation:
Check the event attributes from a single (first) event in a
SampledDataSet. If necessary, clear out previous errors from StatsEngine
prior to saving error, so that the desired error is the only one present
in the data set.

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.validate_transaction_event_sample_data
documentation:
This test depends on values in the test application from
agent_features/test_analytics.py, and is only meant to be run as a
validation with those tests.

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.validate_stats_engine_explain_plan_output_is_none
documentation:
This fixture isn't useful by itself, because you need to generate
explain plans, which doesn't normally occur during record_transaction().

Use the `validate_transaction_slow_sql_count` fixture to force the
generation of slow sql data after record_transaction(), which will run
newrelic.core.stats_engine.explain_plan.

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.validate_error_event_sample_data
documentation:
Validate the data collected for error_events. This test depends on values
in the test application from agent_features/test_analytics.py, and is only
meant to be run as a validation with those tests.

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.validate_transaction_exception_message
documentation: Test exception message encoding/decoding for a single error

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.validate_application_exception_message
documentation: Test exception message encoding/decoding for a single error

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.validate_tt_parenting
documentation:
Validate the parenting and start_time of each node in a transaction trace

expected_parenting is a tuple. The second item is a list of child nodes.
The first item is not used in validation and exists as a tool for the
developer to differentiate the node tuples.

    expected_parenting_example = (
        'TransactionNode', [
            ('FunctionNode', [
                ('FunctionNode', [
                    ('FunctionNode', []),
                    ('FunctionNode', []),
                ]),
        ]),
    ])

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.reset_core_stats_engine
documentation: Reset the StatsEngine and custom StatsEngine of the core application.

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.core_application_stats_engine
documentation:
Return the StatsEngine object from the core application object.

Useful when validating items added outside of a transaction, since
monkey-patching StatsEngine.record_transaction() doesn't work in
those situations.

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.core_application_stats_engine_error
documentation:
Return a single error with the type of error_type, or None.

In the core application StatsEngine, look in StatsEngine.error_data()
and return the first error with the type of error_type. If none found,
return None.

Useful for verifying that application.notice_error() works, since
the error is saved outside of a transaction. Must use a unique error
type per test in a single test file, so that it returns the error you
expect. (If you have 2 tests that record the same type of exception, then
StatsEngine.error_data() will contain 2 errors with the same type, but
this function will always return the first one it finds.)

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.error_is_saved
documentation:
Return True, if an error of a particular type has already been saved.

Before calling application.notice_error() in a test, it's good to
check if that type of error has already been saved, so you know that
there will only be a single example of a type of Error in error_data()
when you verify that the exception was recorded correctly.

Example usage:

    try:
        assert not error_is_saved(ErrorOne)
        raise ErrorOne('error one message')
    except ErrorOne:
        application_instance = application()
        application_instance.notice_error()

    my_error = core_application_stats_engine_error(_error_one_name)
    assert my_error.message == 'error one message'

functions.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.function_not_called
documentation:
Verify that a function is not called.

Assert False, if it is.

classes.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.Environ
documentation: Context manager for setting environment variables temporarily.

classes.name: tests.testing_support.fixtures.TerminatingPopen
documentation: Context manager will terminate process when exiting, instead of waiting.

functions.name: tests.testing_support.mock_external_http_server.simple_get
documentation: This defines an external server test apps can make requests to (instead of
www.google.com for example). This provides 3 features:
1) This removes dependencies on external websites.
2) Provides a better mechanism for making an external call in a test app than
simple calling another endpoint the test app makes available because this
server will not be instrumented meaning we don't have to sort through
transactions to separate the ones created in the test app and the ones
created by an external call.
3) This app runs on a separate thread meaning it won't block the test app.

classes.name: tests.testing_support.mock_external_http_server.MockExternalHTTPHResponseHeadersServer
documentation:
MockExternalHTTPHResponseHeadersServer will send the incoming
request headers back as the response.body, allowing us to validate
httpclient request headers.
